clinical_notes.summary: 79yo male with aortic stenosis (followed 3 years, previously moderate), HTN, and osteoporosis presenting with 2 syncopal episodes during exertion over past month and progressive exertional dyspnea over 3-4 months, now with minimal effort (1 block). Vague substernal pressure with exertion. Narrow pulse pressure noted (BP 134/62). Harsh crescendo-decrescendo systolic murmur grade 4/6 at right upper sternal border radiating to carotids. Pulsus parvus et tardus. Diminished S2. S4 gallop. Allergy: codeine (severe nausea/vomiting).

chest_xray.summary: Mild cardiomegaly with cardiothoracic ratio 0.55. Post-stenotic dilation of the ascending aorta. Calcification of the aortic valve region visible on lateral view. Mild pulmonary vascular congestion without frank edema. No pleural effusions. Lung fields otherwise clear. Findings consistent with severe aortic stenosis with early hemodynamic consequences.

ecg.summary: Normal sinus rhythm at 68 bpm. Left axis deviation. Left ventricular hypertrophy by voltage criteria (Sokolow-Lyon >40mm). Lateral ST-segment depression and T-wave inversions in V4-V6 and I, aVL (LVH strain pattern). PR interval 184ms (borderline first-degree AV block). QRS 98ms. QTc 448ms. No acute ischemic pattern. Pattern consistent with pressure overload from severe AS.

echo.summary: Severe aortic stenosis confirmed: aortic valve area 0.7 cm² (critical), mean gradient 52 mmHg, peak velocity 4.8 m/s. Heavily calcified, trileaflet aortic valve with restricted leaflet motion. Concentric LVH (IVSd 14mm). LVEF preserved at 52%. Diastolic dysfunction grade II. Mild aortic regurgitation. No other significant valvular disease. Mild LA dilation 4.0cm. Post-stenotic aortic root dilation 3.8cm. Findings indicate need for urgent valve replacement evaluation.

heart_sounds.summary: Harsh crescendo-decrescendo systolic ejection murmur grade 4/6 best heard at right upper sternal border, radiating to bilateral carotids. Diminished and delayed S2 (soft A2) consistent with reduced leaflet mobility. S4 gallop at apex due to reduced LV compliance from LVH. Carotid upstroke delayed on palpation (pulsus parvus et tardus). No diastolic murmurs. No pericardial rub.

lab_results.summary: CBC: Hemoglobin 12.8 g/dL (mild anemia of chronic disease — important pre-TAVR evaluation). BMP: creatinine 1.2 mg/dL, eGFR 62 mL/min. BNP: 480 pg/mL (elevated, indicating elevated filling pressures from severe AS). Troponin I: 0.01 ng/mL (normal). Coagulation: INR 1.0, platelets 198 x10³/µL. LDL 96 mg/dL. Calcium 9.4 mg/dL (normal). Pre-procedural workup initiated for TAVR planning.

medication.summary: Active medications: Amlodipine 5mg daily (HTN — use cautiously in severe AS due to vasodilation risk), Hydrochlorothiazide 12.5mg daily (HTN), Alendronate 70mg weekly (osteoporosis), Calcium + Vitamin D daily, Acetaminophen PRN (musculoskeletal pain). Codeine allergy documented. Note: Amlodipine may be relatively contraindicated in severe AS — discuss with cardiology. No anticoagulation currently. Aspirin 81mg daily added for pre-procedural TAVR workup. Urgent cardiac surgery/structural heart team referral placed.
